snippets.title: Distribution plot with histplot and KDE
snippets.code:
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

penguins = sns.load_dataset("penguins")
sns.set_theme()

sns.histplot(data=penguins, x="flipper_length_mm", hue="species", kde=True, multiple="stack")
plt.title("Flipper length distribution by species")
plt.show()
snippets.explanation: `histplot` counts observations per bin; `kde=True` overlays a smooth density estimate. `hue` splits by category; `multiple="stack"` stacks histograms.

snippets.title: Correlation heatmap
snippets.code:
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

iris = sns.load_dataset("iris")
corr = iris.select_dtypes("number").corr()

sns.heatmap(corr, annot=True, fmt=".2f", cmap="vlag", center=0, square=True)
plt.title("Iris feature correlations")
plt.show()
snippets.explanation: Apply `heatmap` to a square correlation matrix. `annot=True` prints coefficients; `center=0` anchors diverging colors at zero correlation.

snippets.title: Pair plot for multivariate EDA
snippets.code:
import seaborn as sns

iris = sns.load_dataset("iris")

sns.pairplot(iris, hue="species", diag_kind="hist", corner=True)
# corner=True omits upper triangle duplicates
snippets.explanation: `pairplot` builds a grid of scatter plots for every numeric pair plus histograms on the diagonal. `hue` colors points by class.

snippets.title: Regression scatter with confidence band
snippets.code:
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

tips = sns.load_dataset("tips")

sns.regplot(data=tips, x="total_bill", y="tip", scatter_kws={"alpha": 0.5})
plt.show()
snippets.explanation: `regplot` fits and draws a linear regression line with a shaded confidence interval— useful for spotting linear trends during EDA.

snippets.title: Faceted categorical comparison with catplot
snippets.code:
import seaborn as sns

tips = sns.load_dataset("tips")

sns.catplot(
    data=tips, kind="box",
    x="day", y="total_bill", hue="sex",
    col="time", height=4, aspect=0.9
)
snippets.explanation: Figure-level `catplot` with `col=` creates small multiples— one box plot panel per time period— comparing bill amounts across days and sex.
